response_raw:
{
"mode": "human",
"topic": "How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.",
"opinion": "To manage and deal with fake content on social media, it's essential to have a critical eye towards the information being shared. Here are some tips to help you identify and avoid fake content: 1) Check the source: Make sure the information is coming from a credible source, and not just a random account or website. Look for sources with a good reputation and fact-checking processes in place. 2) Be cautious of sensational headlines: If a headline seems too good (or bad) to be true, it probably is. Take the time to research the topic further and verify the information through other sources. 3) Use fact-checking websites: Websites like Snopes, FactCheck.org, and PolitiFact can help you verify the accuracy of news articles and claims. 4) Follow verified accounts: Look for accounts that have a blue checkmark next to their name, indicating they've been verified by the platform. This can help ensure you're getting information from real sources. 5) Use privacy settings: On platforms like Facebook and Twitter, you can adjust your privacy settings to limit who can share content with you. This can help reduce the amount of fake content that appears in your feed. 6) Take breaks: If you find yourself feeling overwhelmed or frustrated by fake content on social media, take a break from it for a while. Taking time off can help you recharge and come back to social media with a fresh perspective."
,"reasoning_summary": "To manage and deal with fake content on social media, it's important to have a critical eye towards the information being shared. By checking the source, being cautious of sensational headlines, using fact-checking websites, following verified accounts, adjusting privacy settings, and taking breaks when needed, you can reduce the amount of fake content in your feed and improve your overall social media experience.",

response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.opinion: To manage and deal with fake content on social media, it's essential to have a critical eye towards the information being shared. Here are some tips to help you identify and avoid fake content: 1) Check the source: Make sure the information is coming from a credible source, and not just a random account or website. Look for sources with a good reputation and fact-checking processes in place. 2) Be cautious of sensational headlines: If a headline seems too good (or bad) to be true, it probably is. Take the time to research the topic further and verify the information through other sources. 3) Use fact-checking websites: Websites like Snopes, FactCheck.org, and PolitiFact can help you verify the accuracy of news articles and claims. 4) Follow verified accounts: Look for accounts that have a blue checkmark next to their name, indicating they've been verified by the platform. This can help ensure you're getting information from real sources. 5) Use privacy settings: On platforms like Facebook and Twitter, you can adjust your privacy settings to limit who can share content with you. This can help reduce the amount of fake content that appears in your feed. 6) Take breaks: If you find yourself feeling overwhelmed or frustrated by fake content on social media, take a break from it for a while. Taking time off can help you recharge and come back to social media with a fresh perspective.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: To manage and deal with fake content on social media, it's important to have a critical eye towards the information being shared. By checking the source, being cautious of sensational headlines, using fact-checking websites, following verified accounts, adjusting privacy settings, and taking breaks when needed, you can reduce the amount of fake content in your feed and improve your overall social media experience.
